payload.instructions:
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- Rails app with business logic in `app/controllers`, `app/models`, `app/services`, `app/workers`, and `app/views`; background jobs via Sidekiq.
- Frontend lives in `app/frontend` (Webpacker): entry packs in `app/frontend/packs`, shared components in `app/frontend/components`, namespaces for feature slices, and supporting libs in `app/frontend/lib`.
- Tests: Ruby specs in `spec` (models, requests, services, etc.); JS/React tests run from `app/frontend` and `jest` (mocks/setup) with coverage output to `coverage/`.
- Configuration in `config/` (environment files, initializers, routes); database schema and migrations in `db/`; static assets and compiled outputs in `public/`.

## Setup, Build, and Run
- First time: `bin/setup` (installs gems, yarn packages, prepares database).
- Run app locally: `bundle exec rails s` (Web, http://anedot.me:3000), `bundle exec sidekiq` (jobs), `bin/webpack-dev-server` (hot reloading for frontend).
- Database utilities: `bundle exec rails db:migrate` to apply migrations; `bundle exec rails db:reset` to rebuild from scratch.
- API docs: `RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize` to regenerate Swagger; open with `yarn docs:open:mac` or `yarn docs:open:windows`.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Ruby: enforced by RuboCop (Rails + RSpec cops); 2-space indentation, ~100-char lines, snake_case file names, CamelCase classes/modules. Prefer service objects and POROs in `app/services` for new logic.
- JS/React: ESLint + Prettier defaults; keep shared UI in `app/frontend/components`, feature-specific code under `app/frontend/namespaces/*`. Name hooks `useSomething`, components `PascalCase`, and tests `*.spec.js`/`*.test.js` near the code or under `jest/`.
- Styles: follow existing SCSS/CSS modules and avoid introducing global selectors unless intentional.

## Testing Guidelines
- Backend: `bundle exec rspec`; keep fixtures/factories close to specs and favor request specs for API changes. Use `bundle exec rake database_consistency` when adjusting schemas.
- Frontend: `yarn jest` (collects coverage by default); rely on Testing Library helpers set up in `jest/__setup__`. Mock network calls with `axios-mock-adapter` where possible.
- New features should include happy-path and failure coverage; update swagger specs when API contracts change.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Commits: concise, imperative messages per Tim Pope’s guidance (e.g., “Fix donor validation”), grouped logically.
- PR titles: prefix with a tag such as `[BUGFIX]`, `[FEATURE]`, `[INTERNAL]`, `[MAINTENANCE]`, or `[SECURITY]` followed by a short summary.
- PR body: fill the template (summary, linked issues, screenshots for UI, test/QA checkboxes, cross-browser notes). Label appropriately (e.g., `needs review(s)`, `needs QA`) and request at least one approval. Rebase onto latest `master` before merging to avoid merge bubbles.

# AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/duhl/git/anedot

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- Rails app with business logic in `app/controllers`, `app/models`, `app/services`, `app/workers`, and `app/views`; background jobs via Sidekiq.
- Frontend lives in `app/frontend` (Webpacker): entry packs in `app/frontend/packs`, shared components in `app/frontend/components`, namespaces for feature slices, and supporting libs in `app/frontend/lib`.
- Tests: Ruby specs in `spec` (models, requests, services, etc.); JS/React tests run from `app/frontend` and `jest` (mocks/setup) with coverage output to `coverage/`.
- Configuration in `config/` (environment files, initializers, routes); database schema and migrations in `db/`; static assets and compiled outputs in `public/`.

## Setup, Build, and Run
- First time: `bin/setup` (installs gems, yarn packages, prepares database).
- Run app locally: `bundle exec rails s` (Web, http://anedot.me:3000), `bundle exec sidekiq` (jobs), `bin/webpack-dev-server` (hot reloading for frontend).
- Database utilities: `bundle exec rails db:migrate` to apply migrations; `bundle exec rails db:reset` to rebuild from scratch.
- API docs: `RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize` to regenerate Swagger; open with `yarn docs:open:mac` or `yarn docs:open:windows`.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Ruby: enforced by RuboCop (Rails + RSpec cops); 2-space indentation, ~100-char lines, snake_case file names, CamelCase classes/modules. Prefer service objects and POROs in `app/services` for new logic.
- JS/React: ESLint + Prettier defaults; keep shared UI in `app/frontend/components`, feature-specific code under `app/frontend/namespaces/*`. Name hooks `useSomething`, components `PascalCase`, and tests `*.spec.js`/`*.test.js` near the code or under `jest/`.
- Styles: follow existing SCSS/CSS modules and avoid introducing global selectors unless intentional.

## Testing Guidelines
- Backend: `bundle exec rspec`; keep fixtures/factories close to specs and favor request specs for API changes. Use `bundle exec rake database_consistency` when adjusting schemas.
- Frontend: `yarn jest` (collects coverage by default); rely on Testing Library helpers set up in `jest/__setup__`. Mock network calls with `axios-mock-adapter` where possible.
- New features should include happy-path and failure coverage; update swagger specs when API contracts change.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Commits: concise, imperative messages per Tim Pope’s guidance (e.g., “Fix donor validation”), grouped logically.
- PR titles: prefix with a tag such as `[BUGFIX]`, `[FEATURE]`, `[INTERNAL]`, `[MAINTENANCE]`, or `[SECURITY]` followed by a short summary.
- PR body: fill the template (summary, linked issues, screenshots for UI, test/QA checkboxes, cross-browser notes). Label appropriately (e.g., `needs review(s)`, `needs QA`) and request at least one approval. Rebase onto latest `master` before merging to avoid merge bubbles.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

payload.text:
**Investigating missing database column causing upload errors**

I've identified that the upload error likely stems from a missing database column—probably because migrations weren't run or didn't apply properly. The fix must ensure the migration creating that column is applied; meanwhile, adding the attribute in code won't prevent API strong params from rejecting it. Next, I'll check the controller's create action to see how the attribute is handled.

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Org
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoints for account_documents
      class AccountDocumentsController < BaseController
        before_action :set_document, only: %i[update destroy validate_type]

        def index
          authorize %i[api org v3 account_document]

          documents = @account.documents.page(page).per(per_page)

          serialize_collection(documents)
        end

        def create
          authorize %i[api org v3 account_document]

          document = @account.documents.new(
            account_documents_params.merge(uploaded_by: current_user)
          )

          if document.save
            validate_document(document)
            serialize_obj(document, status: :created)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(document)
          end
        end

        def update
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document]

          if @document.update(account_documents_params)
            validate_document(@document) if revalidate?
            serialize_obj(@document)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(@document)
          end
        end

        def destroy
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document]

          @document.destroy
          head :no_content
        end

        def validate_type
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @document], :validate?

          claimed_type = params[:document_type].presence || params[:documentType].presence
          result = validate_document(@document, claimed_type:, strict: true)
          verdict = result&.[](:verdict)

          render json: {
            id: @document.id,
            documentType: @document.document_type,
            claimedDocumentType: claimed_type || @document.document_type,
            verdict:,
            confidence: result&.[](:confidence),
            reason: result&.[](:reason),
            modelId: result&.[](:model_id),
            validationPassed: @document.validation_passed
          }, status: :ok
        end

      private

        def account_documents_params
          params.require(params_wrapper).permit(
            *WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS.map(&:underscore)
          ).merge(account_id: @account.id)
        end

        def set_account
          @account = current_account_scope.find_by!(uid: params[:account_id])
        end

        def set_document
          @document = @account.documents.find(params[:id])
        end

        def validate_document(document, claimed_type: nil, strict: false)
          unless document.file.present?
            document.update(validation_passed: false)
            return nil
          end

          result = AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator.new(
            document: document,
            claimed_type: claimed_type
          ).call

          document.update(validation_passed: result.fetch(:verdict) == "match")
          result
        rescue StandardError => e
          document.update(validation_passed: false)
          Anedot::ExceptionLogger.warn(e)
          Rails.logger.warn("[AccountDocuments] Validation failed: #{e.class} - #{e.message}")
          nil
        end

        def revalidate?
          @document.saved_change_to_file? || @document.saved_change_to_document_type?
        end

        WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS = %w[
          accountId
          documentType
          documentName
          file
          id
        ].freeze

        wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Public: Documents for Accounts
class AccountDocument < ApplicationRecord
  DOCUMENT_TYPES = %w[
    annual_report
    articles_of_incorporation
    certificate_of_good_standing
    election_filing
    form_1023
    irs_501c
    irs_8976_or_1023
    irs_cp_575
    irs_w9
    tax_document
    voided_check
    meeting_minutes
    vendor_contract
    state_registration_paperwork
    appointment_of_treasurer
    statement_of_organization
    campaign_finance_report
    committee_registration
    state_charity_registration
    irs_8871
  ].freeze

  CATEGORIES = {
    ein: %w[irs_cp_575],
    ownership_affiliation: %w[meeting_minutes articles_of_incorporation annual_report
                              vendor_contract],
    state_incorporation: %w[articles_of_incorporation annual_report state_registration_paperwork],
    nonprofit_determination_letter: %w[irs_501c],
    candidate_or_pac_filing: %w[appointment_of_treasurer statement_of_organization
                                campaign_finance_report committee_registration],
    california_charity_registration: %w[state_charity_registration],
    section_527_pac_designation: %w[irs_8871]
  }.freeze

  enum :status, { unreviewed: 0, verified: 1, rejected: 2, deleted: 3 }

  belongs_to :account
  belongs_to :reviewed_by,
             class_name: "User",
             inverse_of: :reviewed_documents,
             optional: true
  belongs_to :uploaded_by,
             class_name: "User",
             inverse_of: :uploaded_documents,
             optional: true
  mount_base64_uploader :file,
                        DocumentUploader,
                        file_name: lambda { |d|
                          return d.document_type if d.document_name.blank?

                          d.document_name.rpartition(".").first
                        }
  validates :deleted_at,
            presence: true,
            if: -> { deleted? }
  validates :document_type, inclusion: DOCUMENT_TYPES
  validates :status, presence: true

  def deletable?
    unreviewed?
  end
end

**Evaluating attribute persistence vs. DB migration**

The best fix is adding a proper DB column via migration to avoid unknown attribute errors and support persistence, since virtual attributes or attr_accessor won't persist or fully prevent errors during record updates. I'll commit a migration to add the missing column, ensuring the schema and code stay consistent.

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "base64"

RSpec.describe Api::Org::V3::AccountDocumentsController do
  describe "POST /org/v3/account_documents" do
    let(:account) { create(:account, :with_defaults) }
    let(:user) { account_member(account) }
    let(:permission_list) { user.account_users.find_by(account:).permission_list }
    let(:file) { Base64.strict_encode64(fixture("valid_tax_doc.pdf").read) }
    let(:params) do
      {
        accountId: account.uid,
        documentType: "irs_w9",
        documentName: "file.pdf",
        file: "data:application/pdf;base64,#{file}"
      }
    end
    let(:bedrock_client) { instance_double(Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client) }
    let(:bedrock_payload) do
      {
        content: [
          { type: "text", text: { verdict: "match", confidence: 0.91, reason: "Looks like a W-9" }.to_json }
        ]
      }
    end
    let(:bedrock_response) { double(body: StringIO.new(bedrock_payload.to_json)) }

    before do
      permission_list.access.accounts_write = true
      permission_list.save!
      sign_in user
      allow(Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client).to receive(:new).and_return(bedrock_client)
      allow(bedrock_client).to receive(:invoke_model).and_return(bedrock_response)
    end

    it "saves the validation result with the document" do
      post "/org/v3/account_documents",
           params: params,
           as: :json

      expect(response).to have_http_status(:created)
      expect(response.parsed_body).to include(
        "validationPassed" => true
      )
      expect(AccountDocument.last.validation_passed).to eq(true)
    end

    it "sets validation to false when Bedrock fails and succeeds silently" do
      allow(AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator).to receive(:new)
        .and_raise(AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::ValidationError, "boom")

      post "/org/v3/account_documents",
           params: params,
           as: :json

      expect(response).to have_http_status(:created)
      expect(response.parsed_body).to include("validationPassed" => false)
      expect(AccountDocument.last.validation_passed).to eq(false)
    end
  end

  describe "POST /org/v3/account_documents/:id/validate_type" do
    let(:account) { create(:account, :with_defaults) }
    let(:document) { create(:account_document, account: account, document_type: "irs_w9") }
    let(:user) { anedot_admin }
    let(:bedrock_client) { instance_double(Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client) }
    let(:bedrock_payload) do
      {
        content: [
          { type: "text", text: { verdict: "match", confidence: 0.91, reason: "Looks like a W-9" }.to_json }
        ]
      }
    end
    let(:bedrock_response) { double(body: StringIO.new(bedrock_payload.to_json)) }

    before do
      sign_in user
      allow(Aws::BedrockRuntime::Client).to receive(:new).and_return(bedrock_client)
      allow(bedrock_client).to receive(:invoke_model).and_return(bedrock_response)
    end

    it "returns a validation verdict" do
      post "/org/v3/account_documents/#{document.id}/validate_type",
           params: { account_id: account.uid },
           as: :json

      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.parsed_body).to include(
        "id" => document.id,
        "documentType" => "irs_w9",
        "claimedDocumentType" => "irs_w9",
        "verdict" => "match",
        "confidence" => 0.91,
        "reason" => "Looks like a W-9",
        "modelId" => AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::DEFAULT_MODEL_ID,
        "validationPassed" => true
      )

      expect(bedrock_client).to have_received(:invoke_model).with(
        hash_including(
          model_id: AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::DEFAULT_MODEL_ID,
          content_type: "application/json",
          accept: "application/json"
        )
      )
      expect(document.reload.validation_passed).to eq(true)
    end

    it "returns validationPassed false when Bedrock fails but still responds" do
      allow(AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator).to receive(:new)
        .and_raise(AccountDocuments::BedrockValidator::ValidationError, "boom")

      post "/org/v3/account_documents/#{document.id}/validate_type",
           params: { account_id: account.uid },
           as: :json

      expect(response).to have_http_status(:ok)
      expect(response.parsed_body).to include(
        "id" => document.id,
        "documentType" => "irs_w9",
        "validationPassed" => false
      )
      expect(document.reload.validation_passed).to eq(false)
    end
  end
end
